name: Coagulation-Flocculation Treatment
description: Coagulation-flocculation clarifies turbid water: a metal-salt coagulant is rapidly mixed to neutralize colloid surface charge and destabilize particles, then slow mixing promotes collisions that grow flocs; if flocs are large enough they settle out, otherwise aids are added and mixing continues before sedimentation yields clarified water.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Turbid water (colloids)"]
    N2["Add coagulant (Al/Fe salt)"]
    N3["Rapid mix / dispersion"]
    N4["Charge neutralization"]
    N5["Destabilized colloids"]
    N6["Slow mix (flocculation)"]
    N7["Floc growth by collisions"]
    N8{"Floc large + settleable?"}
    N9["Add flocculant aid"]
    N10["Sedimentation"]
    N11["Clarified water"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|no| N9
    N9 -->|more mixing| N6
    N8 -->|yes| N10
    N10 --> N11

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff

    style N10 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N11 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

MWH's Water Treatment: Principles and Design (3rd ed.)
